title: Healthy life expectancy and years lived with disability or disease
subtitle: The [period life expectancy](#dod:period-life-expectancy) at birth, broken down by the estimated number of years lived in "full health" versus years lived with disability or disease burden.
caption:
Healthy life expectancy and years lived with disability or disease

The period life expectancy at birth, broken down by the estimated number of years lived in "full health" versus years lived with disability or disease burden.

This stacked area chart displays global trends in life expectancy from 1990 to 2023, divided into two distinct components. The vertical axis measures time in years, ranging from 0 to 80, while the horizontal axis marks five-year intervals. The lower section, shaded in blue, represents "Healthy life expectancy," while the upper section, shaded in reddish-brown, represents "Years lived with disability." In 1990, the total height of the chart sits just above 60 years, with the blue area accounting for approximately 56 years and the red area comprising the remaining portion. Over the subsequent three decades, the total height of the chart rises steadily, reaching approximately 74 years by 2023. The blue area shows a consistent upward trajectory throughout the period, expanding from the mid-50s to roughly 63 years by the end of the timeline. The red area maintains a relatively stable thickness across the years, hovering between 8 and 11 years, though it exhibits a noticeable dip around 2020 before recovering. By 2023, the combined height of both areas reaches its peak on the chart, with the blue section forming the substantial base and the red section capping the total at just under 75 years.
